references.text: [Er49c] Erd\H{o}s, P., On some applications of {B}run's method. Acta Univ. Szeged. Sect. Sci. Math. (1949), 57--63.

remarks.text: Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er49c} proved this is true for any $c_2>0$ if $c_1>0$ is sufficiently small (depending on $c_1$).

statement.text: Let $c_1,c_2>0$. Is it true that, for any sufficiently large $x$, there exist more than $c_1\log x$ many consecutive primes $\leq x$ such that the difference between any two is $>c_2$?
